places.name: 25th Street Green Space
places.operator: Dogpatch & Northwest Potrero Hill Green Benefit District

places.name: Acta Non Verba - West Oakland Farm Park
Adventure Playground
places.description: Adventure Playground is an urban park and adventure playground in Berkeley, California, located at the Berkeley Marina. The park opened in 1979 based on the ideas of Danish architect Carl Theodor Sørensen, who had made use of scrap junkyards for playgrounds when Copenhagen was under occupation during World War II.

places.name: Alameda County Master Gardeners Lake Merritt Trial Gardens
Albany Beach
places.description: Albany Beach is a sand beach located in Albany, California on the east shore of San Francisco Bay.

places.name: Albany Bulb
places.description: The Albany Bulb is a former landfill largely owned by the City of Albany, in California. The Bulb is the west end of a landfill peninsula jutting west from the east shore of San Francisco Bay.

places.name: Alcatraz and Hyde St Pier ships
Alcatraz Island
places.description: Alcatraz Island is a small island about 1.25 miles offshore from San Francisco in San Francisco Bay, California, near the Golden Gate Strait. The island was developed in the mid-19th century with facilities for a lighthouse, a military fortification, and a military prison.

places.name: Alta Plaza Park
places.description: Alta Plaza Park is a 12.9-acre (5.2 ha) public park in San Francisco, California and caps the top of the western edge of Pacific Heights. It falls under the jurisdiction of the city's Supervisorial District 2. The park is served by several San Francisco Municipal Railway bus lines.

places.name: Alvarado Park
places.description: Alvarado Area is a subsection of Wildcat Canyon Regional Park in Richmond, California and is a National Historic Place. It is located at the mouth of Wildcat Canyon in the Alvarado Area section. Between 1909 and 1923, Alvarado Park was a private park that was owned and operated by local residents.

places.name: Angel Alley
places.operator: Dogpatch & Northwest Potrero Hill Green Benefit District

places.name: Angel Island State Park
aliases: The Angel Island Café and Cantina

places.name: Anthony Chabot Regional Park
places.description: Anthony Chabot Regional Park is a regional park in Alameda County, California, United States. It is part of the East Bay Regional Park District system, and covers 5,067 acres (2,051 ha) in the San Leandro Hills adjacent to Oakland, San Leandro and Castro Valley.

places.name: Aquatic Park
places.description: Aquatic Park is a public park in Berkeley, California, United States, located just east of the Eastshore Freeway between Ashby and University Avenues. The Works Progress Administration created the park in the 1930s simultaneously with the nearby Berkeley Yacht Harbor.

places.name: Aquatic Park Historic District
places.description: Aquatic Park Historic District is a National Historic Landmark and building complex on the San Francisco Bay waterfront within San Francisco Maritime National Historical Park.

places.name: Aramburu Island
places.description: Aramburu Island is a 17-acre (6.9 ha) island in Richardson Bay, Marin County, California. It, along with Strawberry Spit, came to exist in the 1950s and 1960s as a consequence of dumping dredged material from nearby developments into the bay.

places.name: Ashland Grove Park
places.operator: Hayward Area Recreation and Park District

places.name: Baker Beach
places.description: Baker Beach is a public beach on the peninsula of San Francisco, California, United States. The beach lies on the shore of the Pacific Ocean in the West Side of the city.

places.name: Balboa Park
places.description: Balboa Park is a public park in the Mission Terrace neighborhood of San Francisco, California. It was originally dedicated in 1909 when the park included the land now used by City College of San Francisco Ocean Campus west of I-280 Freeway.

places.name: Bay Area Ridge Trail - 02 - East Bay
places.description: The Bay Area Ridge Trail is a multi-use trail along the hill and mountain ridgelines surrounding the San Francisco Bay Area, in Northern California. It is planned to extend 550 miles (890 km); As of May 2025, 427 miles (687 km) have been established.

places.name: Bay Area Ridge Trail - Section 01 - Golden Gate Bridge
Bay Bridge and Treasure Island Viewpoint
Bayview K.C. Jones Playground
aliases: Bayview K.C. Jones Children's Play Area

places.name: Benc⑁ Patisserie Cafe
places.address: 1947 Martin Luther King Jr Way, Berkeley

places.name: Benches Park
places.operator: Dogpatch & Northwest Potrero Hill Green Benefit District

places.name: Berkeley Pier
places.description: The Berkeley Pier is in Berkeley, California. When constructed in 1926, the pier extended 3.5 miles (5.6 km) into San Francisco Bay from the end of University Avenue. Due to extensive filling of the bay and the creation of the Berkeley Marina, it presently extends only 2.5 miles (4.0 km).

places.name: Berkeley Rose Garden

imageCredit.author: Jef Poskanzer from Berkeley, CA, USA

places.description: The Berkeley Rose Garden is a city-owned park in the North Berkeley area of Berkeley, California.

places.name: Bishop Ranch Open Space Regional Preserve
aliases: Bishop Ranch Open Space Regional Preserve
Bishop Ranch Regional Open Space Preserve
places.description: Bishop Ranch Regional Preserve (BRRP), also known as Bishop Ranch Regional Open Space Preserve is a 444-acre (1.80 km2) regional park on a ridge top at the edge of San Ramon, California. It is near a residential area, west of San Ramon Valley Road and south of Bollinger Canyon Road.

places.name: Black Diamond Mines Regional Preserve - Land Bank
Blake Garden
places.description: Blake Garden is a teaching facility for the UC Berkeley College of Environmental Design in the hills of Kensington, California, a census-designated place of the East Bay region of the Bay Area in Northern California, approximately 4 mi (6.4 km) north of the main University of California, Berkeley campus.

places.name: Bohol Circle Immigrant Park
aliases: Bohol Circle Immigrant Park Public Dock

places.name: Bothin Marsh Preserve
places.description: Bothin Marsh is a 106 acres (43 ha) wetland in Marin County, California. Parts of the wetlands are in the Bothin Marsh Open Space Preserve, a regional park.

places.name: Briones Regional Park

imageCredit.author: No machine-readable author provided. Mrbilal1987~commonswiki assumed (based on copyright claims).

places.description: Briones Regional Park is a 6,117-acre (24.75 km2) regional park in the East Bay Regional Park District (EBRPD) system, located in the Briones Hills of central Contra Costa County of the San Francisco Bay Area in California.

places.name: Briones to Mt Diablo Trail
aliases: Briones to Mt. Diablo Regional Trail

places.name: Brooks Island Regional Preserve
places.description: Brooks Island Regional Preserve includes both the 75-acre (30 ha) of Brooks Island above the low-tide line and 300 acres (120 ha) of the surrounding bay. The only public access to the island is via an East Bay Regional Park District naturalist tour.

places.name: Buena Vista Park
places.description: Buena Vista Park is a public park in the Haight-Ashbury and Buena Vista Heights neighborhoods of San Francisco, California. It is the oldest official park in San Francisco, established in 1867 as Hill Park, later renamed Buena Vista.

places.name: Bushrod Park
places.description: The Bushrod neighborhood in North Oakland, Oakland, California, is an area surrounding its namesake park, and bounded by Martin Luther King, Jr. Way to the west, Claremont Avenue to the east, Highway 24 to the south, and the Berkeley border to the north.

places.name: BYA Urban Farm and Community Garden
César Chávez Park
places.description: César Chávez Park is a 90 acres (36 ha) city park of Berkeley, California named after César Chávez. It can be found on the peninsula on the north side of the Berkeley Marina in the San Francisco Bay and is adjacent to Eastshore State Park.

places.name: Cafe Étoile
places.address: 1930 Martin Luther King Jr Way, Berkeley

places.name: Caffe Trieste
places.description: Caffè Trieste is an internationally known coffeehouse, retail store, and former franchise in San Francisco. The original cafe, opened in 1956, was the first espresso-based coffeehouse on the West Coast of the United States.

places.name: Call of the Sea - Brigantine Pier
aliases: Call of the Sea - Brigantine Pier
Call of the Sea - Schooner Pier

places.name: Candlestick Point State Recreation Area
places.operator: California Department of Parks and Recreation
places.description: Candlestick Point State Recreation Area is a state park unit of California, United States, providing an urban protected area on San Francisco Bay. The park is located at the southeastern tip of San Francisco immediately south of Hunters Point and 2 miles (3.2 km) north of Sierra Point in Brisbane.

places.name: Carl Larsen Park
places.description: Carl Larsen Park is a 6.6-acre (2.7 ha) neighborhood park located in the Parkside District on the West Side of San Francisco. It lies west of 19th Avenue, at the intersection with Vicente, and just north of Stern Grove.

places.name: Castro Valley High Scool Garden
places.operator: Castro Valley High Smart Environment Club

places.name: Cayuga Playground

imageCredit.author: Orin Zebest Camera location37° 42′ 49.69″ N, 122° 27′ 00.73″ W View this and other nearby images on: OpenStreetMap 37.713804; -122.450202

places.description: Cayuga Park is a neighborhood park and playground in San Francisco, at the edge of the Cayuga Terrace neighborhood. Its history, location and aesthetics make it unique among the parks of San Francisco.

places.name: Charles Lee Tilden Regional Park
aliases: Charles Lee Tilden Regional Park Botanic Garden
places.description: Charles Lee Tilden Regional Park, also known as Tilden Park or Tilden, [], is a 2,079-acre (841 ha) regional park in the East Bay, part of the San Francisco Bay Area in California. It is between the Berkeley Hills and San Pablo Ridge. Its main entrance is near Kensington, Berkeley, and Richmond.

places.name: Charles Lee Tilden Regional Park Nature Area
Chase Center
places.description: Chase Center is an indoor arena in the Mission Bay neighborhood of San Francisco, California, United States.

places.name: China Basin Park
places.description: China Basin Park is a 5-acre (2.0 ha) park in San Francisco, California, located at the mouth of Mission Creek in the Mission Bay neighborhood and across from the Oracle Park baseball stadium. The park features a hilly lawn, dog run, artificial beach, amphitheater seating, and a statue of Willie McCovey.

places.name: China Camp State Park
places.operator: California Department of Parks and Recreation
places.description: China Camp State Park is a state park in Marin County, California, surrounding a historic Chinese American shrimp-fishing village and a salt marsh. The park is located in San Rafael, California, on the shore of San Pablo Bay. It is known for its hiking and mountain biking trails, scenic views, and open spaces.

places.name: Civic Center Plaza
places.description: Civic Center Plaza, also known as Joseph Alioto Piazza, is the 4.53-acre (1.83 ha) plaza immediately east of San Francisco City Hall in Civic Center, San Francisco, in the U.S. state of California. Civic Center Plaza occupies two blocks bounded by McAllister, Larkin, Grove, and Carlton B.

places.name: Claremont Canyon Regional Preserve
places.description: Claremont Canyon Regional Preserve is a small regional park mainly located in the city of Oakland, California, and administered by the East Bay Regional Park District.

places.name: Clipper Terrace Community Garden
aliases: Crags Court Community Garden West End

places.name: Coit Tower Cafe
places.hours: Apr-Oct Mo-Su 10:00-06:00; Nov-Mar Mo-Su 10:00-05:00; Dec Th[4] off; Dec 25 off; Jan 1 off
places.description: Coit Tower is a 210-foot (64 m) tower in the Telegraph Hill neighborhood of San Francisco, California, overlooking the city and San Francisco Bay. The tower, in the city's Pioneer Park, was built between 1932 and 1933 using Lillie Hitchcock Coit's bequest to beautify the city of San Francisco.

places.name: Commander Isaiah Nelson Memorial Hanging Gardens
Corona Heights Park
places.description: Corona Heights Park is a public park in the Castro and Corona Heights neighborhoods of San Francisco, California, United States. It is situated immediately to the south of Buena Vista Park.

places.name: Crane Cove Park
places.description: Crane Cove Park is a 7-acre (2.8 ha) urban park in Potrero Point, San Francisco, California, located on the city's east-facing waterfront at San Francisco's historic Pier 70. The park opened in 2020. The site used to be a shipbuilding site occupied by the Union Iron Works and the Bethlehem Shipbuilding Corporation.

places.name: Cull Canyon Regional Recreation Area
places.description: Cull Canyon Regional Recreation Area (CCRRA) is a regional park located in Castro Valley, Alameda County, California. It is part of the East Bay Regional Park District (EBRPD) system.

places.name: Diablo Foothills Regional Park
places.description: Diablo Foothills Regional Park is a 1,060 acres (4.3 km2) regional park of the East Bay Regional Park District. It is located in Contra Costa County, in the East Bay region of northern California.

places.name: Dinosaur Hill Park
places.description: Dinosaur Hill Park is a park in Pleasant Hill, California. Located off Taylor Blvd, the park includes 13 acres of open space, hiking, and scenic views. The elevation is 308 feet. There is a small parking area accessible only by northbound traffic.

places.name: Don Biddle Community Park
places.fee: 50 USD registration + 50 USD per year for 1 plot

places.name: Don Castro Regional Recreation Area
places.description: Don Castro Regional Recreation Area is a regional park located in Fairview, California. It is part of the East Bay Regional Parks system.

places.name: Don Fernando Pacheco Adobe
places.description: The Don Fernando Pacheco Adobe is located at 3119 Grant Street in Concord, California.The Fernando Pacheco Adobe is the only Concord landmark listed in all three Historical Registries: entry on the National Register occurred on June 6, 1980; the California Registered landmark number is 455; and the Concord Historical…

places.name: Dr. Aurelia Reinhardt Redwood Regional Park
aliases: Dr. Aurelia Reinhardt Redwood Regional Park
places.description: Dr. Aurelia Reinhardt Redwood Regional Park is a part of the East Bay Regional Parks District (EBRPD) in the San Francisco Bay Area. It is located in the hills east of Oakland, California in Castro Valley, California. The park contains the largest remaining natural stand of coast redwood found in the East Bay.

places.name: Dteamland for Kids Playground at Aquatic Park
Duboce Park
places.description: Duboce Park is a small urban park located between the Duboce Triangle and Lower Haight neighborhoods of San Francisco, California. The park is less than one block wide from north to south and two blocks wide from west to east. Its western boundary is Scott Street, and its eastern boundary is Steiner Street.

places.name: East Bay Skyline National Recreation Trail
Edible Schoolyard
places.description: The Edible Schoolyard (ESY) is a 1-acre (4,000 m2) garden and kitchen program at the Martin Luther King Jr. Middle School, a public middle school in Berkeley, California. It was established in 1995 by chef and author Alice Waters.

places.name: Embarcadero Plaza
places.description: Embarcadero Plaza, previously known as Justin Herman Plaza from its opening in 1972 until 2017, is a 4.1-acre (1.7 ha) plaza near the intersection of Market Street and The Embarcadero in San Francisco's Financial District, in the U.S. state of California.

places.name: Ernie Raimondi Park
places.description: Raimondi Park is a 4,000-seat ballpark and public park in the West Oakland neighborhood of Oakland, California. It is the home field of the Oakland Ballers of the independent Pioneer League, and is closely connected to the history of baseball in Oakland.

places.name: Eugene O'Neill National Historic Site
places.description: The Eugene O'Neill National Historic Site, located in Danville, California, preserves Tao House, the Monterey Colonial hillside home of America's only Nobel Prize-winning playwright, Eugene O'Neill.

places.name: Ferry Building Gate E
places.operator: Golden Gate Bridge, Highway & Transportation District

places.name: Five Canyons Open Space Regional Preserve
aliases: Five Canyons Open Space Regional Preserve

places.name: Flying Pig Bistro

places.name: Fort Funston
places.description: Fort Funston is a former harbor defense installation located on the West Side of San Francisco in the southwestern part of the city.

places.name: Fort Mason
places.description: Fort Mason, in San Francisco, California is a former United States Army post located in the northern Marina District, alongside San Francisco Bay. Fort Mason served as an Army post for more than 100 years, initially as a coastal defense site and subsequently as a military port facility.

places.name: Francisco Park
places.description: Francisco Park is a 4.9-acre (2.0 ha) public park in the Russian Hill neighborhood in San Francisco, California, on the site of a former reservoir. The accessible park has a large lawn, a community garden, a playground, and a dog run. It opened in 2022. It has sweeping views of the San Francisco Bay.

places.name: Frank H. Ogawa Plaza
places.description: Frank H. Ogawa Plaza is a public square located in Downtown Oakland.

places.name: Garfield Square
places.description: Garfield Square, also known as Garfield Park, is a 3.46-acre (14,000 m2) city park located in the Mission District of San Francisco, California. It is bounded by 25th Street to the north, 26th Street to the south, Treat Avenue to the west, and Harrison Street to the east and was first opened in 1884.

places.name: George and Judy Marcus Garden of Enchantment
George Miller Junior Memorial Regional Shoreline
Glen Canyon Park

imageCredit.author: Ian Brown from San Franciso, United States

places.description: Glen Canyon Park is a city park in San Francisco, California. It occupies about 70 acres (28 ha) along a deep canyon adjacent to the Glen Park, Diamond Heights, and Miraloma Park neighborhoods.

places.name: Golden Gate Park
places.description: Golden Gate Park is an urban park between the Richmond and Sunset districts on the West Side of San Francisco, California, United States.

places.name: golden gate park disc golf course front nine
aliases: golden gate park disc golf course front nine
Golden Gate Park Disc Golf Course Back 9

places.name: Golden Gate Park Dog Training Area

Harvey Milk Plaza
places.description: Harvey Milk Plaza is a plaza commemorating Harvey Milk in the Castro District of San Francisco, California, United States. Located at the intersection of Market Street, Castro Street, and 17th Street, it serves as the southern entrance to Castro station.

places.name: Hawk Hill
places.description: Hawk Hill is a 923-foot (281 m) peak in the Marin Headlands, just north of the Golden Gate Bridge and across the Golden Gate strait from San Francisco, California. The hill is within the Golden Gate National Recreation Area.

places.name: Hayes Valley Playground
aliases: Hayes Valley Playground Children's Play Area

places.name: Heather Farm Park
places.description: Heather Farm Park is a 102-acre community park in Walnut Creek, California. It contains the Gardens at Heather Farm, a swimming pool, inclusive playground, sports fields, equestrian center, dog park, fishing pond, nature area, lake, and other amenities.

places.name: Helen Diller Civic Center North Playground
aliases: Helen Diller Civic Center North Playground
Helen Diller Civic Center South Playground

places.name: Heron's Head Park
aliases: Heron's Head Park Nature Exploration Area
places.description: Heron's Head Park is a public park in San Francisco, California; maintained by the San Francisco Port Authority. The park is about 22 acres in size. It is based in a wetland and is known for its bird watching. The park is home to The EcoCenter, an environmental education facility.

places.name: Herz PG Childrens Play Areas;Herz Playground
Hillcrest Knolls Park
places.operator: Hayward Area Recreation and Park District

places.name: Hilltop Park
places.address: 2 Willie B Kennedy Dr, San Francisco

places.name: Holly Park
places.description: Holly Park is a public park in San Francisco, California in the Bernal Heights neighborhood. It is known for excellent views of the city and San Francisco Bay.

places.name: Howard & Langton Mini Park
aliases: Howard & Langton Mini Park Community Garden

places.name: Huntington Park
places.description: Collis P. Huntington Park is a 1.3-acre (0.53 ha) park in the Nob Hill neighborhood of San Francisco, California.

places.name: Hyde Street Pier
places.description: The Hyde Street Pier, at 2905 Hyde Street, is a historic ferry pier located on the northern waterfront of San Francisco in the U.S. state of California.

places.name: J.P. Murphy Playground
aliases: J.P. Murphy Playground Children's Play Area

places.name: Japanese American Alumni Cherry Tree Grove
Japanese Tea Garden
places.address: 75 Hagiwara Tea Garden Drive, San Francisco
places.description: The Japanese Tea Garden in San Francisco, California, is a popular feature of Golden Gate Park, originally built as part of a sprawling World's Fair, the California Midwinter International Exposition of 1894.

places.name: Joaquin Miller Park
places.description: Joaquin Miller Park is a large open space park in the Oakland Hills owned and operated by the city of Oakland, California. It is named after early California writer and poet Joaquin Miller, who bought the land in the 1880s, naming it "The Hights" [sic], and lived in the house preserved as the Joaquin Miller House.

places.name: John Hinkel Park
places.description: John Hinkel Park is an urban park located in the North Berkeley neighborhood of Berkeley, California, U.S. It has been listed by the city as a Berkeley Landmark since April 2, 2001, and it contains a historical plaque since 2003.

places.name: John McLaren Park
places.address: 100 John F Shelley Dr, San Francisco
places.description: John McLaren Park is a park in southeastern San Francisco. At 313.7 acres (126.9 ha), McLaren Park is the fourth largest park in San Francisco by area, after Golden Gate Park, the Presidio, and Lake Merced Park.

places.name: John Muir National Historic Site
places.description: The John Muir National Historic Site is located in the San Francisco Bay Area, in Martinez, Contra Costa County, California.

places.name: Joseph L Alioto Performing Arts Piazza
Keller Beach
places.description: Miller/Knox Regional Shoreline is a 295-acre (1.19 km2) bayside park near the Brickyard Cove neighborhood of the Point Richmond District in Richmond, California.

places.name: Kennedy Grove Regional Recreation Area
places.description: Kennedy Grove Regional Recreation Area (KGRRA), also known simply as Kennedy Grove, is located in Richmond, Contra Costa County, California at the base of San Pablo Dam. Created in 1967, it contains a three-mile hiking trail with an elevation of 760 feet (230 m).

places.name: Lafayette Park
places.description: Lafayette Park is an 12.5 acres (5.1 ha) park in San Francisco, California, United States.

places.name: Lake Chabot Regional Park
places.description: Lake Chabot Regional Park is a regional park located in the southern Berkeley Hills in Alameda County, California. It was opened to the public on June 18, 1966, as part of the East Bay Regional Parks system.

places.name: Lands End
places.description: Lands End is a park in San Francisco within the larger Golden Gate National Recreation Area. It is also the geographic name of a specific promontory next to the Pacific Ocean on the northwest side of the park.

places.name: Lands End Trail Eagles Point, 916 El Camino Del Mar, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

places.name: Las Trampas Wilderness Regional Preserve
places.description: Las Trampas Regional Wilderness is a 5,342-acre (21.62 km2) regional park located in Alameda and Contra Costa counties in Northern California. The nearest city is Danville, California. The park belongs to the East Bay Regional Park District (EBRPD).

places.name: Leona Canyon Open Space Regional Preserve
Levi's Plaza
places.description: Levi Strauss Plaza, also known as Levi Plaza or Levi's Plaza, is an office complex located in North Waterfront district of San Francisco, west of The Embarcadero. It houses the headquarters of Levi Strauss & Co. As of 1998 the company Blue Jeans Equity West is the landlord of the complex.

places.name: Life is Sweet Bakery and Cafe
Lisa and Douglas Goldman Tennis Center
Little Window by Bicycle Banh Mi
Louis Sutter PG Childrens Play Area
Marin Headlands
places.description: The Marin Headlands are a hilly peninsula at the southernmost end of Marin County, California, United States, located just north of San Francisco across the Golden Gate Bridge, which connects the two counties and peninsulas. The entire area is part of the Golden Gate National Recreation Area.

places.name: Marin Islands Ecological Reserve

places.name: Marin Islands National Wildlife Refuge
places.operator: United States Fish and Wildlife Service

McLaughlin Eastshore State Park
places.description: McLaughlin Eastshore State Park is a state park and wildlife refuge along the San Francisco Bay shoreline of the East Bay between the cities of Richmond, Albany, Berkeley, Emeryville, and Oakland. It encompasses remnant natural wetlands, restored wetlands, as well as landfill west of the Eastshore Freeway.

places.name: Metro Milk Tea Hong Kong Dessert
Middle Harbor Shoreline Park
places.description: Middle Harbor Shoreline Park (MHSP) is located on San Francisco Bay and the Oakland Seaport entrance channel, west of downtown Oakland, California. It is owned and operated by the Port of Oakland. The park entrance is at the intersection of 7th Street and Middle Harbor Road. It is open seven days a week from 8 a.m.

places.name: Midtown Terrace Park and Sutro Recreation Center
Minnesota Grove
places.operator: Dogpatch & Northwest Potrero Hill Green Benefit District

places.name: Minnie and Lovie Ward Rec Center
Minnie & Lovie Ward Children’s Play Area
Mission Dolores Park
places.description: Mission Dolores Park, often abbreviated to Dolores Park, is a city park in San Francisco, California. It is located two blocks south of Mission Dolores at the western edge of the Mission District.

places.name: Morcom Rose Garden
places.description: The Morcom Rose Garden is located in a residential neighborhood in Oakland, California, near the Piedmont border. The street address is 700 Jean St., Oakland, CA 94610.

places.name: Moscone Rec Center
places.description: Moscone Recreation Center is a park located between Chestnut Street and Bay Street, Laguna Street and Webster Street in San Francisco's Marina District.

places.name: Mosswood Park
places.description: Mosswood Park is a 4-acre (16,000 m2) public park in Oakland, California, located on W MacArthur Boulevard between Webster Street and Broadway.

places.name: Mount Diablo Gateway
places.operator: Contra Costa County Public Works Department

places.name: Mount Diablo State Park
places.operator: California Department of Parks and Recreation
places.description: Mount Diablo is a mountain of the Diablo Range, in Contra Costa County of the eastern San Francisco Bay Area in Northern California. It is south of Clayton and northeast of Danville. It is an isolated upthrust peak of 3,849 feet, visible from most of the San Francisco Bay Area.

places.name: Mountain Lake Park
places.description: Mountain Lake Park is a 14-acre (5.7 ha) park located in Richmond District on the West Side of San Francisco. Situated north of the intersection of Lake and Funston, the park was designed by engineer William Hammond Hall in the late 19th century.

places.name: Municipal Pier
places.description: Aquatic Park Historic District is a National Historic Landmark and building complex on the San Francisco Bay waterfront within San Francisco Maritime National Historical Park.

places.name: Music Concourse
places.description: The Music Concourse is an open-air plaza within Golden Gate Park in San Francisco. Flanking the oval-shaped concourse are the M. H. de Young Memorial Museum and the California Academy of Sciences.

places.name: MY Coffee Roastery
places.address: 2080 Martin Luther King Jr Way, Berkeley

places.name: My Cup of Tea (Cool Tea Bar)
Noe & Beaver Mini Park
aliases: Noe & Beaver Mini Park Community Garden

places.name: Oakland Arena
places.description: Oakland Arena, often referred to as the Oakland Coliseum Arena, is an indoor arena in Oakland, California, and part of the Oakland–Alameda County Coliseum Authority.

places.name: Ocean Beach
places.description: Ocean Beach is a beach on the west coast of San Francisco, California, United States. Bordering the Pacific Ocean, the beach is 3.5 miles long, and it is known for its strong currents, surf culture, foggy weather in the late spring and summer, and lack of high rise buildings.

places.name: Ohlone Park
places.description: Ohlone Park is a public linear park in the city of Berkeley, California, United States. Directly underground is the subway used by the Bay Area Rapid Transit (BART) Red Line and Orange Line. It is part of the Ohlone Greenway.

places.name: Old Saint Hilary's Open Space Preserve
Oyster Bay Regional Shoreline
places.description: Oyster Bay Regional Shoreline is a park in San Leandro, California, part of the East Bay Regional Park District (EBRPD). It is located along the eastern shore of San Francisco Bay directly to the south of Oakland International Airport.

places.name: Panhandle
places.description: The Panhandle is a public park in San Francisco, California, so named because it forms a panhandle with Golden Gate Park. It is long and narrow, being three-quarters of a mile long and just one block wide.

places.name: Panorama Park
places.operator: San Francisco Recreation and Parks Department

places.name: Pardee Home Museum
places.description: The Pardee Home is a house in Oakland, California. It was the home of three generations of the Pardee family. It is now a non-profit museum showing over 100 years of the life of a prominent California family.

places.name: Peerless Coffee and Tea Espresso and Shop
Pennsylvania Avenue Green Space
places.operator: Dogpatch & Northwest Potrero Hill Green Benefit District

places.name: Philosopher's Way
places.description: Philosopher's Way, San Francisco is a 2.7-mile (4.3 km) loop trail around the perimeter of John McLaren Park created by artists Peter Richards and Susan Schwartzenberg, staff artists at the Exploratorium.

places.name: Philz Coffee
places.address: 403 Corte Madera Town Center, Corte Madera

places.name: Pier 1
places.description: The Central Embarcadero Piers Historic District is a Registered Historic District in the city of San Francisco, California, United States. It consists of Piers 1, 1½, 3 and 5, which form one of the largest surviving pier complexes along San Francisco's Embarcadero waterfront road.

places.name: Pine Lake Park
places.description: Pine Lake Park is a park located on the West Side of San Francisco, which encompasses Pine Lake.

places.name: Pink Triangle Memorial
places.description: The Pink Triangle Park is a triangle-shaped mini-park located in the Castro District of San Francisco, California. The park is less than 4,000 square feet (370 m2) and faces Market Street with 17th Street to its back. The park sits directly above the Castro Street Station of Muni Metro, across from Harvey Milk Plaza.

places.name: Pioneer Park

imageCredit.author: Kyle Harmon from Oakland, CA, USA

places.description: Pioneer Park is a 4.82-acre (1.95 ha) public park crowning the top of Telegraph Hill in San Francisco, California. It was established in 1876 in celebration of the United States Centennial. Prior to establishment of the park, it was the site of the Marine Telegraph Station.

places.name: Point Isabel Regional Shoreline

imageCredit.author: No machine-readable author provided. Theblog assumed (based on copyright claims).

places.description: Point Isabel Regional Shoreline in Richmond, California, is operated by East Bay Regional Park District, and is a multi-use park for joggers, windsurfers, kayakers, photographers, picnickers, and people walking dogs. It has access for pedestrians and via public transit, private vehicles, and bikes.

places.name: Point Molate Beach Park
places.description: Point Molate Beach Park is a city-owned park in Richmond, California, accessible only via a narrow road from the Richmond-San Rafael Bridge toll plaza exit "Western Drive", and separate from the rest of the city. The park is adjacent to the present day ghost town of Winehaven.

places.name: Point Pinole Regional Shoreline
places.description: Point Pinole Regional Shoreline is a regional park on the shores of the San Pablo Bay, California, in the United States. It is approximately 2,315 acres (9.37 km2) in area, and is operated by the East Bay Regional Park District.

places.name: Portsmouth Square
places.description: Washington Square is an 2.8-acre (1.1 ha) park in the North Beach district of San Francisco. It was established in 1847 and is one of the city's first parks.

places.name: Potrero del Sol
places.description: Potrero del Sol Park, San Francisco Potrero del Sol Park, formally known as “La Raza Park,” is an urban park located at the intersection of Potrero Avenue and 25th St in the Calle 24 Latino Cultural District within San Francisco’s Mission District.

places.name: Presidio of San Francisco
places.operator: Presidio Trust;National Park Service;Golden Gate National Parks Conservancy
places.description: The Presidio of San Francisco is a park and former U.S. Army post on the northern tip of the San Francisco Peninsula in San Francisco, California, and is part of the Golden Gate National Recreation Area.

places.name: Progress Park
places.operator: Dogpatch & Northwest Potrero Hill Green Benefit District

places.name: Radio Beach
places.description: The Radio Beach Shoreline is the only beach in Oakland, California. On the San Francisco Bay, it borders the onramp to the Bay Bridge, leading into San Francisco. Its name refers to the radio towers on the beach.

places.name: Red Bay Coffee
places.hours: Wednesday - Monday: 7:30 am - 4 pm, Tuesday: 7:30 am - 5:00 pm

places.name: Regional Parks Botanic Garden
places.description: The Regional Parks Botanic Garden is a 10-acre botanical garden located in Tilden Regional Park in the Berkeley Hills, east of Berkeley, California, in the United States. It showcases California native plants, and is open to the public. The garden was founded on January 1, 1940.

places.name: Ridgeview Open Space
places.operator: Pleasant Hill Recreation and Park District

places.name: Ritual Coffee Roasters
places.description: Ritual Coffee Roasters is a coffee roaster based in San Francisco, California, with six cafes in San Francisco and Napa. Founded in 2005, Ritual Roasters is considered part of the "third wave of coffee" independent companies which both roast their own beans, wholesale, and operate cafes.

places.name: Robert Sibley Volcanic Regional Preserve
places.description: Robert Sibley Volcanic Regional Preserve is located in the Oakland Hills of the East Bay region of the San Francisco Bay Area, California. The park is part of the East Bay Regional Parks District (EBRPD), covers 928 acres (3.76 km2), and lies east of Oakland, partly in Alameda County and partly in Contra Costa County.

places.name: Robert W. Crown Memorial State Beach
places.description: Crown Memorial State Beach is a state park in the city of Alameda, California on the shores of San Francisco Bay. It is operated by East Bay Regional Parks District (EBRPD), under a cooperative agreement with the State of California and the City of Alameda.

places.name: Round House Café
places.description: Round House Café is a cafe and diner in the Presidio of San Francisco, California next to the Golden Gate Bridge.

places.name: Russell Research Station
places.operator: University of California, Berkeley, Division of Agriculture and Natural Resources

places.name: Ruth Bancroft Garden
places.description: The Ruth Bancroft Garden is a 3.5-acre (1.4 ha) public dry garden established by Ruth Bancroft. It contains more than 2,000 cactus, succulents, trees, and shrubs native to California, Mexico, Chile, South Africa, and Australia. It is located at 1552 Bancroft Road in Walnut Creek, California, USA.

places.name: San Bruno Mountain State and County Park
places.description: San Bruno Mountain State Park, officially San Bruno Mountain State and County Park, is a park located in northern San Mateo County, California. It is adjacent to the southern boundary of San Francisco and borders the cities of Brisbane, South San Francisco, Colma and Daly City.

places.name: San Francisco Botanical Garden
places.operator: San Francisco Recreation & Parks Department;San Francisco Botanical Garden Society
places.description: The San Francisco Botanical Garden at Strybing Arboretum is located in San Francisco's Golden Gate Park.

San Francisco Crosstown Trail - Hiking - Section 2

Sigmund Stern Recreation Grove
places.description: Sigmund Stern Recreation Grove, locally called Stern Grove, is a 33-acre (130,000 m2) recreational site located on the West Side of the city of San Francisco California USA in the Parkside District.

places.name: Signal Point Park
places.operator: San Francisco Recreation and Parks Department

places.name: Splash Pad Park
places.description: Splash Pad Park is the name of several parks containing a splash pad. The original is in Oakland, California, along Lake Park Ave, between Grand Ave and Lakeshore Ave, and north of the 580 Freeway.

places.name: St. Mary's Square
places.description: Saint Mary's Square is a park and urban square across California Street from Old St. Mary's Cathedral in San Francisco's Chinatown, in the U.S. state of California.

places.name: Starbucks
places.address: 1544 Palos Verdes Mall, Palos Verdes Mall, Walnut Creek

places.name: Sue Bierman Park
aliases: Waterfront Playground at Sue Bierman Park
places.description: Sue Bierman Park, previously known as Ferry Park, is a 4.5-acre (1.8 ha) park in San Francisco, California in the Financial District, in the U.S. state of California. The park is named after Sue Bierman, a San Francisco civic activist and San Francisco Supervisor.

places.name: Sunset Dunes
places.description: Sunset Dunes is a 2 mi (3.2 km) long public urban park next to Ocean Beach on the West Side of San Francisco, California. The park is located on a former location of the Great Highway between Lincoln Way and Sloat Boulevard.

places.name: Sutro Heights Park
places.description: Sutro Heights Park is an historic public park in the Outer Richmond District located on the West Side of San Francisco, California. It is within the Golden Gate National Recreation Area and the Sutro Historic District.

places.name: Sycamore Valley Open Space Regional Preserve
places.description: Sycamore Valley Regional Open Space Preserve is a regional park in Contra Costa County, California, outside of Danville, California, United States that is part of the East Bay Regional Parks (EBRPD) system.

places.name: Sydney G. Walton Square

imageCredit.author: Original uploader was Ванка5 at bg.wikipedia

places.description: Sydney Walton Square is a hybrid public and private park located just west of the Embarcadero in San Francisco, California, United States. The park is named after San Francisco's Sydney Grant Walton, a banker and former vice chairman of the San Francisco Redevelopment Agency.

places.name: Tank Hill
places.description: Tank Hill Park is a municipal park in the Ashbury Heights neighborhood of San Francisco, California. The hill is named for a water tank that formerly stood there, and was acquired for park use in 1977.

places.name: Temescal Regional Recreation Area
places.description: Temescal Regional Recreation Area (TRRA), formerly Lake Temescal Regional Park, is a regional park in the Berkeley Hills, in northeastern Oakland, California.

places.name: The Barbary Coast Trail
places.description: The Barbary Coast Trail is a marked trail that connects a series of historic sites and several local history museums in San Francisco, California. Approximately 180 bronze medallions and arrows embedded in the sidewalk mark the 3.8-mile (6.1 km) trail.

places.name: The Den By Craftsman And Wolves
The Gears
places.operator: Dogpatch & Northwest Potrero Hill Green Benefit District

places.name: The Little Spot Cafe
places.address: 1199 South Van Ness Avenue, San Francisco

places.name: Tightwad Hill
places.description: Tightwad Hill is the popular name for Charter Hill, the hill rising to the east of California Memorial Stadium at the University of California, Berkeley in Berkeley, California, U.S.

places.name: Toll Plaza Beach
places.description: The Radio Beach Shoreline is the only beach in Oakland, California. On the San Francisco Bay, it borders the onramp to the Bay Bridge, leading into San Francisco. Its name refers to the radio towers on the beach.

places.name: Top of the Mark
places.hours: We-Sa 17:00-21:30 open "appetizers available" || We-Sa 16:30-23:30 open; Su,Mo-Tu off

imageCredit.author: Travis Wise from Bay Area, California, United States

places.description: The Top of the Mark is a penthouse level bar located on the nineteenth floor of the Mark Hopkins Hotel on Nob Hill at California and Mason Streets in San Francisco, California.

places.name: Torpedo Wharf
places.description: Torpedo Wharf is a wharf in the Presidio of San Francisco, in the U.S. state of California. The site has been a wharf since 1854, and earned its current name when the United States Army built a naval mine depot c. 1907–1909. The current wharf was established in 1941, and now serves as a popular tourist destination.

places.name: University of California Botanical Garden at Berkeley
places.description: The University of California Botanical Garden is a 34-acre botanical garden located on the University of California, Berkeley campus, in Strawberry Canyon. The garden is in the Berkeley Hills, inside the city boundary of Oakland, with views overlooking the San Francisco Bay.

places.name: View on Golden Gate Bridge & Richardson Bay
Visitacion Valley Greenway
aliases: Visitacion Valley Greenway Children’s Play Area

aliases: Wag World Dog Park - Large Dogs
Wag World Dog Park - Small Dogs

places.name: Warm Water Cove Park
places.description: Warm Water Cove is an outdoor, formerly industrialized picnic area in San Francisco, California, located near Pier 80 and the Dogpatch neighborhood.

imageCredit.author: Michael Fraley from San Francisco, US

places.name: Wildcat Canyon Regional Park
places.description: Wildcat Canyon Regional Park is a 2,789-acre (1,129 ha) East Bay Regional Park District (EBRPD) park bordering the city of Richmond in Contra Costa County in the San Francisco Bay Area of California.

places.name: Wildcat Creek Restoration and Greenway Trail
William Joseph McInnes Botanic Garden and Arboretum
Yacht Harbor and Marina Green
places.description: The Marina Green in San Francisco, California, is a 74-acre (300,000 m2) expanse of grass between Fort Mason and the Presidio. It is adjacent to San Francisco Bay, and this location provides good views of the Golden Gate Bridge, Angel Island, Alcatraz Island, and parts of Marin County.

places.name: Yerba Buena Gardens
places.description: Yerba Buena Gardens is the name for two blocks of public parks located between Third and Fourth, Mission and Folsom Streets in the South of Market (SoMA) neighbourhood of San Francisco, California. The first block bordered by Mission and Howard Streets was opened on October 11, 1993.

places.name: Yik Oi Huang Peace & Friendship Park
aliases: Yik Oi Huang Peace and Friendship Park
Yik Oi Huang Peace & Friendship Park
